tasks.description: Create `useWaveformData` hook at `reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useWaveformData.ts`. Fetch audio/video file via `fetch()` + Web Audio API `decodeAudioData()`. Downsample decoded PCM buffer into `numBuckets` RMS amplitude values (each 0-1, normalized to peak). Respect `from`/`to` trim and `speed` to only analyze the audible portion. Cache decoded `AudioBuffer` by URL in a module-level `Map<string, Promise<AudioBuffer>>`. Use `useEffect` with AbortController for cleanup. If `src` is undefined, fetch fails, or `decodeAudioData` rejects (e.g., video without audio stream), return `{ waveform: null, loading: false }` gracefully. Clamp minimum buckets to 2. Signature: `useWaveformData(src: string | undefined, options: { from?: number; to?: number; speed?: number; numBuckets: number }) => { waveform: number[] | null; loading: boolean }`. Reference `AudioAnalysisProvider.tsx:233-243` for the decode pattern but do NOT couple to it. Target ~80 lines.

Create `WaveformOverlay` component at `reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/WaveformOverlay.tsx`. Takes `waveform: number[]` prop and renders a mirrored SVG waveform (top half + bottom half). Absolutely positioned (`inset-0`, `pointer-events-none`), semi-transparent fill (`opacity-20` to `opacity-30`, `fill="currentColor"`). Generate a single `<path>` element with M/L commands. Use `preserveAspectRatio="none"` so CSS scales to clip dimensions. Memoize the path string via `useMemo`. Target ~100 lines. Ensure clip label/icon remain readable behind the overlay.

Wire waveform into `ClipAction.tsx` at `reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx`. Add `audioSrc?: string` and `clipWidth?: number` to `ClipActionProps`. Inside the clip button, call `useWaveformData(audioSrc, { from: clipMeta.from, to: clipMeta.to, speed: clipMeta.speed, numBuckets: Math.max(2, Math.floor((clipWidth ?? 60) / 3)) })`. Render `<WaveformOverlay waveform={waveform} />` when waveform is non-null, positioned absolutely behind existing content (z-index below label). Update `areClipActionPropsEqual` to compare `audioSrc` and `clipWidth`. Eligibility rules (enforced by parent, but documented here): audio track clips show waveform unless `volume === 0`; visual track clips show waveform only if asset type starts with `video/` AND `volume > 0`; text/effect clips never show waveform.

Pass `audioSrc` and `clipWidth` from `TimelineEditor.tsx` at `reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx`. In the `getActionRender` callback: look up the clip from `resolvedConfig` by `action.id`, look up the track by `clip.track`. Apply eligibility rules: if `track.kind === 'audio'` and `clip.volume !== 0` and `clip.assetEntry?.src` → pass `audioSrc`. If `track.kind === 'visual'` and `clip.assetEntry?.type?.startsWith('video/')` and `(clip.volume ?? 1) > 0` and `clip.assetEntry?.src` → pass `audioSrc`. Otherwise `audioSrc = undefined`. Compute `clipWidth` from `(action.end - action.start) * pixelsPerSecond`. Check the `getActionRender` type signature in `types/timeline-canvas.ts` — extend it to pass computed width if needed (preferred: `getActionRender(action, row, width)`). If the signature change is too invasive, pass `pixelsPerSecond` as a prop to ClipAction instead.

Run existing tests and verify. Run `npx vitest run` (or the project's test command) on video-editor tests to ensure no regressions. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until green. Then write a short throwaway verification script that imports `useWaveformData` with a mock/test audio URL and confirms it returns a non-null waveform array of the expected length — run it, confirm it works, then delete it. Document manual smoke test checklist results: (1) audio clip → waveform appears, (2) video clip with audio → waveform appears on visual track, (3) detach audio → waveform disappears from visual clip and appears on new audio clip, (4) text/effect clip → no waveform, (5) zoom timeline → waveform re-renders with appropriate bucket count, (6) trim clip → waveform shows trimmed portion, (7) mute clip (volume: 0) → waveform disappears, (8) 10+ audio clips → no visible jank.

watch_items: FLAG-001: Clips with volume === 0 (especially post-detach visual clips) must NOT show waveforms. The detachAudioFromVideo util (editor-utils.ts:188-245) sets volume: 0 on the visual clip. Verify this path explicitly.

FLAG-002: decodeAudioData on video files may fail silently if no audio stream exists. The hook MUST return null gracefully — no thrown errors, no broken UI. Video-on-visual-track waveforms are 'should' not 'must'.

ASSUMPTION: The timeline is div-based (ClipAction is a <button>), NOT canvas-based despite the file being named TimelineCanvas.tsx. Verify before implementing — if it IS canvas, SVG approach won't work.
ASSUMPTION: getActionRender callback signature can be extended. Check types/timeline-canvas.ts first. If extending is too invasive, fall back to passing pixelsPerSecond as a prop.
ASSUMPTION: AssetRegistryEntry.type contains MIME strings like 'video/mp4', 'audio/mpeg'. Verify the actual shape before writing eligibility logic.
PERFORMANCE: AudioBuffer cache is module-level Map<string, Promise<AudioBuffer>>. Ensure it doesn't leak memory with many unique URLs. Consider a bounded cache if needed.
DEBT: Do not introduce render-time side effects (see existing debt item video-editor-effect-registry). The useWaveformData hook must only trigger fetches inside useEffect, not during render.
VISUAL: Row height is assumed ~32-40px. If significantly smaller, the mirrored waveform may be unreadable. Check actual height and adjust opacity/style accordingly.
POINTER-EVENTS: WaveformOverlay must have pointer-events: none so it doesn't intercept clip click/drag/resize/context-menu interactions.

sense_checks.question: Does useWaveformData correctly handle all failure modes: undefined src, failed fetch, failed decodeAudioData (video without audio), zero-length buffer? Does it return { waveform: null, loading: false } in each case without throwing?
Is WaveformOverlay truly non-interactive (pointer-events: none) and does it render behind the clip label/icon without obscuring them? Is the SVG path memoized?
Does ClipAction only render the waveform when audioSrc is truthy, and does areClipActionPropsEqual correctly compare the new props to prevent unnecessary re-renders?

Are the eligibility rules correctly implemented: audio tracks with volume > 0 get waveforms, visual tracks only if MIME starts with 'video/' AND volume > 0, text/effect tracks never? Does detach-audio flow result in waveform moving from visual clip to new audio clip?
Do all existing video-editor tests pass? Has the detach-audio → waveform-moves scenario been verified? Is there no visible jank with multiple audio clips?

meta_commentary:
Key execution guidance:

1. **Read before writing.** Before each task, read the target files thoroughly. The plan references specific line numbers and patterns (e.g., AudioAnalysisProvider.tsx:233-243 for the decode pattern, editor-utils.ts:188-245 for detach logic) — verify these are still accurate.

2. **Timeline is div-based, not canvas.** Despite the filename TimelineCanvas.tsx, clips are rendered as `<button>` elements via ClipAction. The SVG overlay approach depends on this. If the executor discovers it IS actually canvas-rendered, stop and reassess — the entire visual approach would need to change.

3. **T1 and T2 are fully parallel** — no shared dependencies. Execute them simultaneously. T3 depends on both. T4 depends on T3. T5 is final verification.

4. **The getActionRender signature extension (T4) is the riskiest integration point.** Check how many callers use this callback. If it's called from multiple places, adding a `width` parameter is safe (existing callers just ignore it). If the type is strict and shared, prefer Option B (pass pixelsPerSecond to ClipAction).

5. **Volume === 0 gating is critical.** This is the core of FLAG-001. The detach-audio workflow creates a muted visual clip and a new audio clip from the same source. If volume gating is wrong, users see duplicate waveforms or waveforms on silent clips. Test this flow explicitly.

6. **No new dependencies.** Use Web Audio API's `decodeAudioData` directly. Do NOT add `@remotion/media-utils` or any other package.

7. **Cache cleanup:** The module-level Map cache persists for the page lifetime. For a video editor this is fine (bounded by project assets), but if you notice it growing unboundedly, add an LRU or WeakRef strategy.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Create useWaveformData hook with Web Audio API decodeAudioData, caching, trim/speed support, and graceful failure
Step 2: Create WaveformOverlay SVG component with mirrored waveform, absolute positioning, pointer-events none
Step 3: Wire waveform into ClipAction with audioSrc/clipWidth props, eligibility rules, and areClipActionPropsEqual update
Step 4: Pass audioSrc and clipWidth from TimelineEditor, resolve eligibility per clip, extend getActionRender signature
Step 5: Run existing tests, manual verification of all scenarios including detach-audio flow and performance

validation.completeness_notes: All 5 plan steps are mapped 1:1 to finalize tasks. T1/T2 parallel, T3 depends on both, T4 depends on T3, T5 is final verification. The plan's execution order is preserved exactly.
